Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == Prefecture Cultural Properties database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Hirao Town website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Hirao Town website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Hirao Town website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Hirao Town website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Hirao Town website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Hirao Town website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Hirao Town website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Tumulus (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Tumulus length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | Construction = [[5th century]] (early) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | Buried Person = | Artifacts = Numerous grave goods | Historic Designation = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Cultural Properties = Excavated artifacts (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) | Notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | Map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}-period burial mound located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. Shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}, it is designated as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Tumulus length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳}} (Yanai City): Tumulus length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳}} (Shimonoseki City): Tumulus length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} or a local chieftain. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Tumulus length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Cultural Properties == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Shiratori Kofun Excavated Artifacts (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Cultural Properties database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun Artifacts] (Hirao Town website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}}
