Kofun (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Kofun length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | built = [[5th century]] (early) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|12=draft}}) | Buried Person = | burial goods = Numerous grave goods | alternate_name = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Historic Site = excavations (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|12=draft}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|12=draft}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|12=draft}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|12=draft}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|12=draft}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|12=draft}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun Hirao}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun Hirao}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Kofun (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Kofun length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | built = [[5th century]] (early) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Buried Person = | burial goods = Numerous grave goods | alternate_name = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Historic Site = excavations (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|12=draft|12=draft}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|12=draft|12=draft}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|12=draft|12=draft}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun Hirao}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|12=draft|12=draft}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|12=draft|12=draft}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|12=draft|12=draft}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun Hirao}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun Hirao}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Kofun (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Kofun length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | built = [[5th century]] (early) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin||qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Buried Person = | burial goods = Numerous grave goods | alternate_name = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Historic Site = excavations (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun Hirao}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

revision.comment: 27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町) up to rev 99995313
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 白鳥古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地 = [[山口県]][[熊毛郡 (山口県)|熊毛郡]][[平生町]]佐賀 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 53|緯度秒 = 49.64 |経度度 =132|経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 47.83 |ISO = JP-35 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長120m
高さ11m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]（内部に[[箱式石棺]]か） |被葬者 = |出土品 = 副葬品多数 |史跡 = 山口県指定史跡「白鳥古墳 付 陪塚及び周濠」 |有形文化財 = 出土品（山口県指定文化財） |特記事項 = 山口県第1位の規模 |地図2 ={{Location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=57|lat_sec=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|label=[[柳井茶臼山古墳|柳井茶臼山
古墳]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南南西.svg|marksize=19}} {{Location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=0|lat_sec=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|label=[[仁馬山古墳|仁馬山
古墳]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-西北西.svg|marksize=19}} {{Location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|label='''白鳥古墳'''|label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=前方後円墳-北北西.svg|marksize=20}}}} }} '''白鳥古墳'''（しらとりこふん）は、[[山口県]][[熊毛郡 (山口県)|熊毛郡]][[平生町]]佐賀にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。山口県指定史跡に指定され、出土品は山口県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 山口県では最大規模の古墳で山口県における主な古墳は次の通り。 # '''白鳥古墳'''（熊毛郡平生町佐賀） - 周防地方。墳丘長120メートル。 # [[柳井茶臼山古墳]]（柳井市柳井） - 周防地方。墳丘長80メートル。 # [[仁馬山古墳]]（下関市延行） - 長門地方。墳丘長75メートル。 、[[5世紀]]前半頃（[[古墳時代]]中期）の築造と推定される。 == 概要 == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|墳丘上に鎮座する白鳥神社}}]] 山口県東部で平生町の中央部、[[室津半島]]西岸の小丘陵の先端部に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。現在は後円部の中央には白鳥神社が鎮座し、その造営の際に墳丘が掘り取られている{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北北西方に向ける{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。墳丘は3段築成史跡説明板。。墳丘長は120メートルを測り、瀬戸内地方西部（山口県・広島県域）では最大規模になる{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[埴輪]]片（[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]）が検出されている{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。また墳丘周囲には、周濠（幅15-20メートル{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}）および[[陪塚]]が認められる{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]であったとされ[http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/home/html/bunkazai/jomon.html#jo9 古墳]（平生町ホームページ）。{{リンク切れ|date=2018-05-15}}、[[江戸時代]]の白鳥神社再建時に石室内の「石櫃」（[[箱式石棺]]か）から多くの副葬品が出土したといい、それらは現在に伝世される。 この白鳥古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、瀬戸内海西部に勢力を持った豪族（熊毛の王、[[周防国造|周芳国造]]一族か）の首長墓と想定される{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。周辺では本古墳のほかにも、[[柳井茶臼山古墳]]（柳井市柳井、前代首長墓）や阿多田古墳・[[神花山古墳]]（いずれも平生町、後代首長墓）などの築造が知られる。 古墳域は[[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）に陪塚・周濠とともに山口県指定史跡に指定され、出土品は[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に山口県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[寛文]]2年（[[1749年]]）、発掘と遺物出土{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、弘津史文による踏査。前方後円墳と判明{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。 * [[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）1月12日、山口県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1973年]]（昭和48年）、平生町史編纂に伴う測量調査。規模が判明（広島大学）{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）3月24日、出土品が山口県指定有形文化財に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 白鳥古墳　付陪塚及び周濠]（山口県ホームページ「山口県の文化財」）。。 * 墳丘長：120メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：64メートル ** 高さ：11メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：60メートル ** 高さ：8.5メートル 墳丘周囲に巡らされた周濠は、幅20.8メートル・深さ2.1メートルを測る。 File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), koenbu.JPG|後円部
{{small|墳丘を削って後円部中央に白鳥神社本殿が鎮座する。}} File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), zenpoubu.JPG|後円部から前方部を望む == 出土品 == [[ファイル:白鳥古墳 (平生町) 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|白鳥古墳出土品}}{{small|画像中の埴輪片以外は複製品。平生町歴史民俗資料館展示。}}]] 出土品について「注進案」によれば、[[寛延]]元年（[[1748年]]）に白鳥神社社殿が大風で大破し、その再建の際に社殿跡地を掘ると多くの遺物が出土したという{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。それらは白鳥神社の社宝とされ現在に伝わっている{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。出土品は次の通り。 * 二神二獣鏡 1面 *: 面径18センチメートルの[[仿製鏡]]。平縁で、外区は複波文帯・[[鋸歯文]]帯、内区には4個の乳による区画に二神二獣を配する。 * 四神四獣鏡 1面 *: 面径13.2センチメートルの仿製鏡。平縁で、外区は複波文様・鋸歯文帯、内区は8個の乳による区画に四神四獣を配する。 * 巴形銅器 5個 * 鉄斧頭 5個 * 鉄刀 3片 * 鉄鋤先 1片 * 管玉 11個 * [[形象埴輪]]片 1片 これらは「白鳥古墳出土品」として一括して山口県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 陪塚 == [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), baicho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|陪塚}}]] [[陪塚]]（陪冢）としては、後円部の南西約18メートルに1基が存在する{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。長軸約24メートル、短軸約14メートルの長方形状で、高さは約2.3メートル{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。内部は未調査であり明らかでない{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。なお、陪塚ではなく白鳥古墳の周堤帯の一部と見る説もある{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。 == 文化財 == === 山口県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 白鳥古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 明細は以下。所有者は白鳥神社。1981年（昭和56年）3月24日指定[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 白鳥古墳出土品]（山口県ホームページ「山口県の文化財」、文化財要録）。。 *** 二神二獣鏡 1面 *** 四神四獣鏡 1面 *** 巴形銅器 5個 *** 鉄斧頭 5個 *** 鉄刀 3片 *** 鉄鋤先 1片 *** 管玉 11個 *** 形象埴輪片 1片 * 史跡 ** 白鳥古墳 付 陪塚及び周濠 - 1971年（昭和46年）1月12日指定[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=10040 白鳥古墳　付陪塚及び周濠]（山口県ホームページ「山口県の文化財」、文化財要録）。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[山口県]][[熊毛郡 (山口県)|熊毛郡]][[平生町]]佐賀 '''交通アクセス''' * [[柳井駅]]（[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[山陽本線]]）から、[[防長交通|防長交通バス]]で「宮の下」バス停下車 '''関連施設''' * 平生町歴史民俗資料館 - 白鳥古墳の出土品の一部を保管・展示。 '''周辺''' * [[神花山古墳]] - 山口県指定史跡。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=白鳥古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 36 山口県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=白鳥古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|白鳥古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=白鳥古墳|title=山口県史 資料編 考古1|publisher=山口県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[周防国造]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 白鳥古墳　付陪塚及び周濠]、[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 白鳥古墳出土品] - 山口県ホームページ「山口県の文化財」 * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html 白鳥古墳]、[http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html 白鳥古墳出土品] - 平生町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりこふん ひらおちよう}} [[Category:山口県の古墳]] [[Category:平生町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:山口県指定史跡]]

周防地方。墳丘長120メートル。 # [[柳井茶臼山古墳]]（柳井市柳井） - 周防地方。墳丘長80メートル。 # [[仁馬山古墳]]（下関市延行） - 長門地方。墳丘長75メートル。 、[[5世紀]]前半頃（[[古墳時代]]中期）の築造と推定される。 == 概要 == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|墳丘上に鎮座する白鳥神社}}]] 山口県東部で平生町の中央部、[[室津半島]]西岸の小丘陵の先端部に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。現在は後円部の中央には白鳥神社が鎮座し、その造営の際に墳丘が掘り取られている{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北北西方に向ける{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。墳丘は3段築成史跡説明板。。墳丘長は120メートルを測り、瀬戸内地方西部（山口県・広島県域）では最大規模になる{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[埴輪]]片（[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]）が検出されている{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。また墳丘周囲には、周濠（幅15-20メートル{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}）および[[陪塚]]が認められる{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]であったとされ[http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/home/html/bunkazai/jomon.html#jo9 古墳]（平生町ホームページ）。{{リンク切れ|date=2018-05-15}}、[[江戸時代]]の白鳥神社再建時に石室内の「石櫃」（[[箱式石棺]]か）から多くの副葬品が出土したといい、それらは現在に伝世される。 この白鳥古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、瀬戸内海西部に勢力を持った豪族（熊毛の王、[[周防国造|周芳国造]]一族か）の首長墓と想定される{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。周辺では本古墳のほかにも、[[柳井茶臼山古墳]]（柳井市柳井、前代首長墓）や阿多田古墳・[[神花山古墳]]（いずれも平生町、後代首長墓）などの築造が知られる。 古墳域は[[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）に陪塚・周濠とともに山口県指定史跡に指定され、出土品は[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に山口県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[寛文]]2年（[[1749年]]）、発掘と遺物出土{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、弘津史文による踏査。前方後円墳と判明{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。 * [[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）1月12日、山口県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1973年]]（昭和48年）、平生町史編纂に伴う測量調査。規模が判明（広島大学）{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）3月24日、出土品が山口県指定有形文化財に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 白鳥古墳　付陪塚及び周濠]（山口県ホームページ「山口県の文化財」）。。 * 墳丘長：120メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：64メートル ** 高さ：11メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：60メートル ** 高さ：8.5メートル 墳丘周囲に巡らされた周濠は、幅20.8メートル・深さ2.1メートルを測る。 File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), koenbu.JPG|後円部
{{small|墳丘を削って後円部中央に白鳥神社本殿が鎮座する。}} File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), zenpoubu.JPG|後円部から前方部を望む == 出土品 == [[ファイル:白鳥古墳 (平生町) 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|白鳥古墳出土品}}{{small|画像中の埴輪片以外は複製品。平生町歴史民俗資料館展示。}}]] 出土品について「注進案」によれば、[[寛延]]元年（[[1748年]]）に白鳥神社社殿が大風で大破し、その再建の際に社殿跡地を掘ると多くの遺物が出土したという{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。それらは白鳥神社の社宝とされ現在に伝わっている{{Sfn|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}。出土品は次の通り。 * 二神二獣鏡 1面 *: 面径18センチメートルの[[仿製鏡]]。平縁で、外区は複波文帯・[[鋸歯文]]帯、内区には4個の乳による区画に二神二獣を配する。 * 四神四獣鏡 1面 *: 面径13.2センチメートルの仿製鏡。平縁で、外区は複波文様・鋸歯文帯、内区は8個の乳による区画に四神四獣を配する。 * 巴形銅器 5個 * 鉄斧頭 5個 * 鉄刀 3片 * 鉄鋤先 1片 * 管玉 11個 * [[形象埴輪]]片 1片 これらは「白鳥古墳出土品」として一括して山口県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 陪塚 == [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), baicho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|陪塚}}]] [[陪塚]]（陪冢）としては、後円部の南西約18メートルに1基が存在する{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。長軸約24メートル、短軸約14メートルの長方形状で、高さは約2.3メートル{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。内部は未調査であり明らかでない{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。なお、陪塚ではなく白鳥古墳の周堤帯の一部と見る説もある{{Sfn|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000|pp=412-416}}。 == 文化財 == === 山口県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 白鳥古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 明細は以下。所有者は白鳥神社。1981年（昭和56年）3月24日指定[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 白鳥古墳出土品]（山口県ホームページ「山口県の文化財」、文化財要録）。。 *** 二神二獣鏡 1面 *** 四神四獣鏡 1面 *** 巴形銅器 5個 *** 鉄斧頭 5個 *** 鉄刀 3片 *** 鉄鋤先 1片 *** 管玉 11個 *** 形象埴輪片 1片 * 史跡 ** 白鳥古墳 付 陪塚及び周濠 - 1971年（昭和46年）1月12日指定[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=10040 白鳥古墳　付陪塚及び周濠]（山口県ホームページ「山口県の文化財」、文化財要録）。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[山口県]][[熊毛郡 (山口県)|熊毛郡]][[平生町]]佐賀 '''交通アクセス''' * [[柳井駅]]（[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[山陽本線]]）から、[[防長交通|防長交通バス]]で「宮の下」バス停下車 '''関連施設''' * 平生町歴史民俗資料館 - 白鳥古墳の出土品の一部を保管・展示。 '''周辺''' * [[神花山古墳]] - 山口県指定史跡。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=白鳥古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 36 山口県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=白鳥古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|白鳥古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=白鳥古墳|title=山口県史 資料編 考古1|publisher=山口県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|山口県史 資料編 考古1|2000}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[周防国造]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 白鳥古墳　付陪塚及び周濠]、[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 白鳥古墳出土品] - 山口県ホームページ「山口県の文化財」 * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html 白鳥古墳]、[http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html 白鳥古墳出土品] - 平生町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:しらとりこふん ひらおちよう}} [[Category:山口県の古墳]] [[Category:平生町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:山口県指定史跡]]

Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun Hirao}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}}

designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} {{citation needed|Shiratori Kofun Hirao}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

Kofun (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Kofun length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | built = [[5th century]] (early) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin||qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Buried Person = | burial goods = Numerous grave goods | alternate_name = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Historic Site = excavations (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label={{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label={{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nimayama Kofun|qq=}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumage District, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nimayama Kofun|qq=}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label={{ill|Jinbayama Kofun|lt=Jinbayama Kofun|lt=Nimayama Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumage District, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Jinbayama Kofun|lt=Jinbayama Kofun|lt=Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nimayama Kofun|qq=}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label={{ill|Jinbayama Kofun|lt=Jinbayama Kofun|lt=Nimayama
