Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumage District, Yamaguchi|ja|熊毛郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumage District, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|柳井茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Jinbayama Kofun|lt=Jinbayama Kofun|lt=Nimayama Kofun|ja|仁馬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nimayama Kofun|qq=}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao, Yamaguchi|ja|平生町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]

It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:山口県の古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Kofun (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Kofun length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | built = [[5th century]] (early) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | Buried Person = | burial goods = Numerous grave goods | alternate_name = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Historic Site = excavations (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label=[[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label=[[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]-period Kofun located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. shaped as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # [[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]] (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # [[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]] (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:山口県指定史跡]] [[Category:平生町]]

While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]]

Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label=[[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label=[[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]-period Kofun located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. shaped as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # [[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]] (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # [[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]] (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]

sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | Buried Person = | burial goods = Numerous grave goods | alternate_name = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Historic Site = excavations (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label=[[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label=[[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]-period Kofun located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. shaped as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # [[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]] (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # [[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]] (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]

Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[:en:1971|1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Kofun (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Kofun length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | built = [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (early) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | Buried Person = | burial goods = Numerous grave goods | alternate_name = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Historic Site = excavations (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label=[[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label=[[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]-period Kofun located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. shaped as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # [[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]] (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # [[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]] (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[:en:1981|1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[:en:1971|1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]

(possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | Buried Person = | burial goods = Numerous grave goods | alternate_name = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Historic Site = excavations (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label=[[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label=[[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]-period Kofun located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. shaped as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # [[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]] (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # [[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]] (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[:en:1981|1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[:en:1971|1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]

Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[:en:1971|1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shiratori Kofun | image = [[File:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Shimane), funkyu.JPG|280px]]
Kofun (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | location = [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|49.64|N|132|4|47.83|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Kofun length: 120 m
Height: 11 m (posterior section) | built = [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (early) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly containing a {{ill|Box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | Buried Person = | burial goods = Numerous grave goods | alternate_name = Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site ("Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and surrounding moat") | Historic Site = excavations (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) | notes = Largest tumulus in Yamaguchi Prefecture | map = {{location map+|Japan Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label=[[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label=[[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]-period Kofun located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. shaped as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # [[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]] (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # [[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]] (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[March 24]], [[:en:1981|1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[January 12]], [[:en:1971|1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]

Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label=[[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-southsouthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=34|coordinates=0|coordinates=49.87|lon_deg=130|lon_min=56|lon_sec=31.93|map_dot_label=[[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun-westnorthwest.svg|marksize=19}} {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]-period Kofun located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. shaped as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # [[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]] (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # [[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]] (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[:en:March 24|March 24]], [[:en:1981|1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[:en:January 12|January 12]], [[:en:1971|1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]

Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[:en:January 12|January 12]], [[:en:1971|1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]

Yamaguchi|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=57|coordinates=56.29|lon_deg=132|lon_min=7|lon_sec=44.39|map_dot_label=[[:en:Yanai Yamaguchi|coordinates=33|coordinates=53|coordinates=49.64|lon_deg=132|lon_min=4|lon_sec=47.83|map_dot_label='''Shiratori Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpokoenfun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]-period Kofun located in [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Kumage District|Kumage District, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. shaped as a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # [[:en:Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun]] (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # [[:en:Jinbayama Kofun|Nimayama Kofun]] (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central [[:en:Hirao|Hirao, Yamaguchi]], on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|qq=}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on [[:en:March 24|March 24]], [[:en:1981|1981]].[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on [[:en:January 12|January 12]], [[:en:1971|1971]]. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Hirao)]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]]
