Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=bottom|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun-northnorthwest.svg|marksize=20}} }} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}-period Kofun located in {{ill|Hirao|en|Hirao|WD=Q1205194|lt=Hirao, Yamaguchi|1=Hirao}}, {{ill|Kumage District|en|Kumage District|WD=Q841375|lt=Kumage District, Yamaguchi|1=Kumage District}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. shaped as a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}, it is Historic Site1 as a Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site, and its excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as Historic Site by the prefecture. This tumulus is the largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture.Significant kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture include: # '''Shiratori Kofun''' (Hirao, Kumage District): Kofun length of 120 meters. # {{ill|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11534089|lt=Yanai Chausuyama Kofun|1=Yanai Chausuyama Kofun}} (Yanai City): Kofun length of 80 meters. # {{ill|Jinbayama Kofun|en|Jinbayama Kofun|WD=Q24866215|lt=Nimayama Kofun|1=Jinbayama Kofun}} (Shimonoseki City): Kofun length of 75 meters. It is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the [[Middle Kofun period]]. == Overview == [[File:Shiratori-jinja (Hirao, Shimane), haiden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shiratori Shrine, located atop the tumulus}}]] Shiratori Kofun is located in eastern Yamaguchi Prefecture, in central {{ill|Hirao|en|Hirao|WD=Q1205194|lt=Hirao, Yamaguchi|1=Hirao}}, on the western shore of the {{ill|Murotsu Peninsula|ja|室津半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murotsu Peninsula|1=Murotsu Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568637}}, at the tip of a small hill. At present, Shiratori Shrine stands atop the posterior section of the tumulus, having partially damaged the mound during its construction.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the anterior section facing north-northwest.Explanatory plaque on-site. The tumulus is built in three tiers and measures 120 meters in length, making it the largest in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region (Yamaguchi and Hiroshima Prefectures). The exterior features {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat measuring 15–20 meters in width, as well as an associated tumulus. The burial facility is believed to be a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, and during the reconstruction of Shiratori Shrine in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, numerous grave goods were reportedly found. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century, during the Middle Kofun period. While the identity of the interred is unknown, it is thought to have been a chief of a powerful clan in the western [[Seto Inland Sea]] region, possibly the {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a local chieftain. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Kofun length: 120 meters * Posterior section (3 tiers): ** Diameter: 64 meters ** Height: 11 meters * Anterior section (3 tiers): ** Width: 60 meters ** Height: 8.5 meters The surrounding moat is 20.8 meters wide and 2.1 meters deep. == Historic Site == === Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun burial goods (Archaeological Materials) - Designated on {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|WD=Q2453|lt=March 24|1=March 24}}, {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database). Includes: *** One two-deity-two-beast mirror *** One four-deity-four-beast mirror *** Five comma-shaped bronze objects *** Five iron axes *** Three fragments of iron swords *** One fragment of an iron spade *** Eleven tubular beads *** One figurative haniwa fragment * Historic Site ** Shiratori Kofun with attendant tumuli and moat - Designated on {{ill|January 12|en|January 12|WD=Q2248|lt=January 12|1=January 12}}, {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}}. == notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Suō Kokuzō|ja|周防国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Kokuzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} * [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=10040 Shiratori Kofun] and [http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=90046 Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Yamaguchi Prefecture Historic Site database) * [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399310632.html Shiratori Kofun] and [http://www.town.hirao.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1485399376812.html Shiratori Kofun burial goods] (Hirao Town website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (平生町)|version=99995313|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E7%99%BD%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:白鳥古墳 (平生町)]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiratori Kofun (Hirao, Yamaguchi)}} [[zh:白鳥古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q22119041}} [[Category:Hirao, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
